_note: Anonymized social-signal aggregate. Desk labels are anonymized; no creator names. Paid-membership content excluded. Educational use only — not investment advice.

signals.thesis: Initiated a bullish call option position on Microsoft after a recent price dip.
signals.entry: bought MSFT calls today on the dip
signals.context: Creator reports buying Microsoft call options after a price dip — a short-term bullish options trade.

signals.thesis: In weak markets, defensive actions outperform aggressive bets, so prioritize protection over offense.
signals.entry: Favor defensive positioning and risk controls during market downturns rather than seeking aggressive gains.
signals.context: During bad markets, adopt defensive strategies as the primary approach instead of aggressive moves.

signals.thesis: When a market trend breaks, halt adding to positions and shift focus to protecting existing capital.
signals.entry: Stop scaling into positions when the trend reverses; implement protective measures such as hedges or tighter risk controls.
signals.context: If the trend breaks, stop adding and prioritize protection of capital over new exposure.

signals.thesis: Sell puts only on equities you would be happy to own to ensure comfortable assignment outcomes.
signals.entry: Write cash-secured puts on stocks you genuinely want to own if assigned (strike & expiration not specified).
signals.context: Prefer selling puts only on names you'd want to hold to avoid being stuck with undesirable assignments.

signals.thesis: Always keep a cash buffer so you never face full exposure during market stress.
signals.entry: Maintain at least 30% cash allocation in portfolio at all times.
signals.context: Keep a minimum 30% cash reserve to avoid being fully invested into market drawdowns.

signals.thesis: Concentrate capital into a few high‑conviction LEAP positions instead of holding many small, low‑conviction ones.
signals.entry: Close the majority of low‑conviction LEAPs and redeploy size into the top ~5 LEAP positions.
signals.context: Advised a student to close 35 of 40 LEAPs and build five larger positions, producing outsized returns.

signals.thesis: As an alternative to buying LEAPs, selling puts on Tempest AI allows collecting premium and potentially acquiring the stock at a lower price while betting on upside.
signals.catalyst: TEM exhibits similar IV characteristics to other names and a bullish technical setup
signals.entry: Sell ATM or OTM puts to collect premium or set an entry price for assignment
targets: Premium collected; assignment at a discount to current price
signals.context: Sell puts on TEM as an income/assignment strategy instead of buying LEAPs outright.

signals.thesis: Selling puts on a 2x utilities ETF offers a lower‑volatility income path to participate in the utilities rally versus buying LEAPs outright.
signals.catalyst: recent fast weekly gains in leveraged utilities and attractive option premium
signals.entry: Sell ATM or OTM puts as cash‑secured income trades depending on risk tolerance
targets: Collect premium / own leveraged utilities at a discount if assigned
signals.context: Use UTLS put selling to generate income and potentially buy leveraged utilities at a discount.

signals.thesis: Selling puts on the 2x Uber ETF monetizes elevated IV and either produces income or allows acquisition of leveraged Uber exposure at a discount.
signals.catalyst: high implied volatility (~70%) on the leveraged ETF and doubled upside when Uber rallies
signals.entry: Sell cash‑secured puts (ATM or OTM) depending on desired assignment level
targets: Collect premium; generate steady income or be assigned leveraged shares at a discount
signals.context: Sell puts on UBRL to earn high premium and/or obtain leveraged Uber exposure if assigned.

signals.thesis: Tempest AI shows a monthly bullish engulfing candle and consolidation breakout potential, offering large upside for LEAP buyers in a volatile small‑cap AI/healthcare name.
signals.catalyst: monthly engulfing candle, institutional/ARC buying and a clear runway for growth
signals.entry: Buy LEAP calls; aggressive example cited is a 75 strike LEAP
targets: Large multiple returns (300–500% possible if stock rallies strongly)
signals.context: Tempest AI has an engulfing monthly candle and runway; buying LEAPs (e.g., 75 strike) could see big multiples.

signals.thesis: Uber has consolidated for months and shows activation signals that could drive a push to a new all‑time high, making long‑dated calls appealing.
signals.catalyst: multi‑month consolidation, bullish engulfing-like price action and channel activation
signals.entry: Buy ~350‑day LEAP calls; consider 85 strike or 95 strike depending on risk/target
targets: Push to a new all‑time high
95 strike as a profit target example (200%+ LEAP potential)
signals.context: Uber back in activation; buy 350d LEAPs (85–95 strikes) targeting a move to new highs.

signals.thesis: Southern Company formed a bullish monthly engulfing candle suggesting likely follow‑through higher over coming months.
signals.catalyst: monthly engulfing candlestick at a support/activation level
signals.entry: Buy shares on current levels or on defined pullbacks (no specific price given)
targets: Expect one-to-two monthly candles higher (near-term continuation)
signals.context: SO has an engulfing monthly candle indicating likely gains over the next 1–2 months.

signals.thesis: Utilities ETF is showing a bottom/momentum signal amid sector rotation and should push to new highs, making LEAPs attractive.
signals.catalyst: momentum indicator flashing bottom; rising energy demand (data centers) and macro rotation into utilities
signals.entry: Buy LEAP calls ~300 days out, roughly 10% out‑of‑the‑money (systemic LEAP picker)
signals.context: Thinks XLU just started a move; buy 300d ~10% OTM LEAPs aiming for ~$48–$50.

warnings.thesis: The creator notes GLD saw its first sizable transaction in months — the 11th largest print — taking place around potential technical demand.
warnings.context: First sizable GLD transaction in months; 11th largest print near potential technical demand.

warnings.thesis: MicroStrategy issued a major announcement that may affect its shares and related bitcoin flows, warranting monitoring.
warnings.catalyst: recent unspecified company announcement by MicroStrategy
warnings.context: MicroStrategy made a big announcement; watch flow movements this week for potential market impact.

warnings.thesis: Recent bitcoin-native fund outflows were severe and could continue to influence price via flow dynamics.
warnings.catalyst: last week's large outflows from bitcoin-native products
warnings.context: Noted brutal outflows from bitcoin-native products; monitor flows this week for price impact.

warnings.thesis: Health-care ETF XLV has been the top-performing major sector over the past five trading days while other names have started to show strength.
warnings.context: XLV led major sectors over the past five days; other names have begun to show strength.

warnings.thesis: Crypto cycles indicate a bottom around October; Ethereum is positioned to bottom soon and start a multi‑year bull market, so scale into positions now.
warnings.catalyst: 4‑year crypto cycle pattern, historical bottoms timing, and anticipated institutional/regulatory adoption
warnings.entry: Begin scaling in now and continue building position over the next ~60 days toward the expected October bottom
targets: Multi‑year bull market gains over the next 3–4 years
warnings.context: Calls an October crypto cycle bottom; recommends scaling into Ethereum for a 3–4 year bull market.
